sourcePaper.title: Histone post-translational modifications - cause and consequence of genome function
sourcePaper.sourcePdf: workspace/cell_papers/2022_NRG_Histone post-translational__modifications — cause and__consequence of genome function.pdf

boundedMetadata.mainClaimHint: Histone modifications can act as causes, consequences, reinforcing signals, or memory-like records depending on timing, perturbation, and mechanism evidence.
boundedMetadata.copyrightBoundary: Only title-level bibliographic metadata and short paraphrased rubric hints are stored; no abstract or body text is reproduced.

minimumInputs: candidate histone mark and genomic process
perturbation evidence for writer, reader, eraser, or local mark editing
rescue or orthogonal validation evidence when available
planChecklist: Classify each claim as cause, consequence, reinforcement, memory mark, or unresolved.
Score whether the mark precedes, follows, or co-occurs with the measured process.
Check whether perturbing the mark machinery changes the process in the predicted direction.
Distinguish local direct evidence from global indirect or viability effects.
List common confounders before assigning a strong causal verdict.
Emit a reusable rubric that contains no paper-specific special cases.
sensitivityChecks: Re-score claims when perturbation evidence is removed.
Separate writer perturbation from direct mark editing evidence.
Flag claims that rely only on colocalization or expression correlation.
Track whether evidence comes from the same cell state and time window.
negativeResultChecks: If perturbation changes many unrelated chromatin features, weaken direct causality.
If timing evidence shows the mark appears after the process, classify as consequence or reinforcement.
If rescue evidence is absent for a strong causal claim, require a partial verdict.
rubricPrompts: Use the same rubric for PRDM9/DSB fate and SETD1B broad H3K4me3 attempts.
Separate association strength from causal direction.
Preserve negative or uncertain outcomes as valid scientific results.
